name: Al Cohn
abstract: Alvin Gilbert Cohn (November 24, 1925 – February 15, 1988) was an American jazz saxophonist, arranger and composer. He came to prominence in the band of clarinetist Woody Herman and was known for his longtime musical partnership with fellow saxophonist Zoot Sims.

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: Al Cohn, Zoot Sims — I could go on all day — Tina Brooks, Hank Mobley. You know they play one note and you know who they are immediately.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Lateano
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Al Cohn, he was a favorite, man I loved that guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Lateano interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: And then that would be the band where we started with the three tenors and baritone exclusively. >> [(note)] Al Cohn [INTERVIEWEE] And so Hal Cohen was there and Zoot.

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: we worked together with the Bird Land All Stars. We went out tour for ten weeks... the group that I was involved with was Phil Woods, Kenny Dorham, Al Cohn and myself

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Kenny Durham and uh uh oh dear dear dear, uh Count, Conte Candoli, Kenny Durham, Al Cohn and myself. We played with Sarah Vaughan’s rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: It was Al Cohn and myself representing the East Coast... And that was my first real tour... And I heard a voice, “back here, Phil” and it was Al Cohn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999
